name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 12.01.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 12.01.2026.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 09, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 644.29 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 626.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on January 09, 2026, the area sown under Rabi crops is 644.29 lakh ha (including 89.36 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 95.88 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 626.64 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 09.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India.
texts.text:  As on 09.01.2026, 99% of area has been harvested for Kharif crops all over India.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature :  Minimum temperatures are likely to be above  normal  2-4°C over Western Himalayan Region and northeast India, these are likely to be near normal over plains of northwest India & adjoining central India. These are likely to be below normal by 2-4°C over East & adjoining Central India during the week.
texts.text: Temperature :  Minimum temperatures are likely to be above  normal  2-4°C over Western Himalayan Region and northeast India, these are likely to be near normal over plains of northwest India & adjoining central India. These are likely to be below normal by 2-4°C over East & adjoining Central India during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 07 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  73%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  74%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  79%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  98%;  and South Peninsula: 24%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From January 01- January 07 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is  73%  lower  than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  & Northeast  India:  (-)  74%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  (-)  79%;  (iii)  Central  India:  (-)  98%;  and South Peninsula: 24%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A  fresh  Western  disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  Western  Himalayan  region  from  15th January, 2025.
texts.text:  A  fresh  Western  disturbance  is  likely  to  affect  Western  Himalayan  region  from  15th January, 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heavy rainfall very likely at isolated places over Tamil Nadu on 12th January, 2026.
texts.text:  Heavy rainfall very likely at isolated places over Tamil Nadu on 12th January, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered to Fairly widespread activity likely over Western Himalayan Region, Plains of northwest India except Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh where isolated/scattered rainfall activity is likely.
texts.text:  Scattered to Fairly widespread activity likely over Western Himalayan Region, Plains of northwest India except Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh where isolated/scattered rainfall activity is likely.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated rainfall activity is likely over Kerala, Lakshadweep and Nicobar Islands.
texts.text:  Isolated rainfall activity is likely over Kerala, Lakshadweep and Nicobar Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold wave to Severe Cold wave conditions very likely to continue over Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana,  Delhi,  Himachal  Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand  on  12th  &  13th  with  cold  wave conditions in isolated pockets on 14th January with reduction thereafter.
texts.text:  Cold wave to Severe Cold wave conditions very likely to continue over Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana,  Delhi,  Himachal  Pradesh  and  Uttarakhand  on  12th  &  13th  with  cold  wave conditions in isolated pockets on 14th January with reduction thereafter.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense fog conditions very likely to continue during morning hours over northwest India and Bihar during next 5 day.
texts.text:  Dense fog conditions very likely to continue during morning hours over northwest India and Bihar during next 5 day.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold day conditions likely to prevail in isolated parts of Bihar during 11th -16th January and over North Rajasthan on 11th January.
texts.text:  Cold day conditions likely to prevail in isolated parts of Bihar during 11th -16th January and over North Rajasthan on 11th January.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water Reservoir Status : As on 08 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 138.832 billion cubic meter, which is 75.6%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 106.42 percent of last year's storage and 122.60 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status : As on 08 January 2026, the total live water storage in 166 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 138.832 billion cubic meter, which is 75.6%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's  storage  is  nearly 106.42 percent of last year's storage and 122.60 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels :  In  August  2025,  In  Post-monsoon  2025, around  90% of the country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper  water  levels  of  more  than  20  m  are  observed  in  around  5%  of  stations  of  the country  covering  mainly  the  western  states,  especially  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh  during  Post-monsoon  2025  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.
texts.text:  Annual  water  level  comparison  with  the  previous  year  Post-monsoon  2024  to  Postmonsoon  2025  has  shown  that  about  63%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country experienced rise in  annual  water level fluctuation may  be  attributed to the less groundwater extraction compared to 2024.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.
texts.text:  About 73% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  Postmonsoon 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in Post-monsoon  2025 ,  groundwater conditions  across  India  remained  largely  stable,  with 91%  of  monitoring  stations showing no  deficit .  However,  localized  deficits  were  observed  in  several  regions  notably in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and the Dadar Nagar Havili & Daman & Diu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers : The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (01 st Jan - 07 th Jan 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 73%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Jan - 07 th Jan 2025) is lower than LPA by 73%.
texts.text:  During the week (01 st Jan - 07 th Jan 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 73%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Jan - 07 th Jan 2025) is lower than LPA by 73%.

texts.orig:  Minimum Temperature is likely to be below normal in most of India except J&K, some parts of Himachal Pradesh, Saurashtra-Kutch, some parts of South Karnataka and NE India.

texts.text: Week 2 (16 Jan - 22 Jan 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Minimum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  2-4°C  over  Western  Himalayan Region and northeast India, these are likely to be near normal over plains of northwest India &  adjoining  central  India.  These  are  likely  to  be  below  normal  by  2-4°C  over  East  & adjoining Central India during the week.
texts.text:  Minimum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  2-4°C  over  Western  Himalayan Region and northeast India, these are likely to be near normal over plains of northwest India &  adjoining  central  India.  These  are  likely  to  be  below  normal  by  2-4°C  over  East  & adjoining Central India during the week.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text:  Cold  wave  conditions  likely  to  occur  at  isolated  pockets  of  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand  and Odisha during some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text:  Wheat: About  334.17  lakh  ha  (107%  of  normal)  area  coverage  under  Wheat  has  been reported as compared to 328.04 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.text:  Rapeseed & Mustard : About 89.36 (113% of normal) lakh ha area coverage under Rapeseed &  Mustard  has  been  reported  as  compared  to  86.57  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding period of last year.
